chief_complaint: A 30-year-old female patient with epilepsy continued to experience reading-induced focal seizures with preserved consciousness despite being on levetiracetam 3,000 mg/day.
history: The patient had a history of reading-induced seizures, which were resistant to levetiracetam. She was administered intranasal seletracetam (SEL) to prevent reflex seizures.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Electroencephalogram (EEG) showed spike-frequency decline dose-dependently (3.1/min at placebo to 1.6/min after second dose), with reduced spike-propagation on magnetoencephalography (MEG).
correct_diagnosis: Intranasal seletracetam (SEL) for prevention of reflex seizures
differentials: Intranasal seletracetam (SEL) for prevention of reflex seizures
correct_ranking: Intranasal seletracetam (SEL) for prevention of reflex seizures
